title: Type 1 MI — atherothrombotic cascade
subtype: Type 1 (atherothrombotic) myocardial infarction: plaque rupture/erosion → coronary thrombosis → myocardial injury
description: Ordered biological steps from lipid retention in the arterial wall through plaque destabilization, luminal thrombosis, and the shared myocardial-injury endpoint. Molecules are placed at the step where their mechanistic role is strongest. An off-pathway/systemic bucket holds molecules that are markers of MI but act on a separate axis (Type 2 supply–demand, generic acute-phase, renal/hemodynamic, cardiomyocyte-injury-only).

steps.name: Lipid retention & oxidation
steps.description: ApoB-lipoprotein retention in the subendothelial intima and their oxidative modification — the initiating substrate of atherogenesis.

steps.name: Plaque inflammation
steps.description: Monocyte/macrophage recruitment and vascular inflammation that destabilize the atherosclerotic plaque; includes leukocyte adhesion, cytokines, and oxidative enzymes.

steps.name: Fibrous-cap degradation & rupture
steps.description: Matrix-metalloproteinase-driven degradation of the fibrous cap extracellular matrix, cap thinning and rupture exposing the thrombogenic lipid core.

steps.name: Endothelial activation & erosion
steps.description: Endothelial dysfunction, activation and superficial erosion (the non-rupture mechanism of Type 1 MI), exposing subendothelial collagen/von Willebrand factor and adhesion molecules.

steps.name: Platelet adhesion & activation
steps.description: Platelet tethering to exposed collagen/vWF, activation, α-granule release and platelet–leukocyte crosstalk at the injured surface.

steps.name: Thromboxane amplification
steps.description: COX-1/thromboxane-A2 generation amplifying platelet recruitment and aggregation (the aspirin-sensitive step).

steps.name: Coagulation & thrombus formation
steps.description: Tissue-factor-triggered coagulation, thrombin generation and fibrin polymerization forming the occlusive coronary thrombus; fibrinolytic turnover markers.

steps.name: Myocardial injury (shared endpoint)
steps.description: Cardiomyocyte ischemia and necrosis — the shared endpoint that troponin measures for both MI types; includes early-release injury markers.

steps.name: Off-pathway / systemic markers
steps.description: Molecules associated with MI but acting on a separate axis: Type 2 supply–demand (natriuretic peptides, lactate), renal/hemodynamic stress, generic acute-phase, or neurohormonal — shown for completeness, not placed on the atherothrombotic cascade.
